rulings.Case Title: BURLINGAME INVESTMENT CORPORATION et al VS. HERMAN KWAI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 29, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT RAFAEL PACQUING'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The motion was continued for the sole purpose of the issuance of a ruling. The court has now determined that the motion should be granted as to the seventh cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage and denied as to the third cause of action for publication of injurious falsehood, fourth cause of action for slander of title, the fifth cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and the sixth cause of action for intentional interference with contract. A claim for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage requires proof that the alleged economic relationship was disrupted. There is no such evidence here. As to all other claims, there are triable issues whether defendant engaged in and/or conspired with others to engage in the wrongdoing alleged, whether the communications made by defendants were made in good faith and without malice, whether those communications made the contractual relations alleged in the sixth claim more expensive or difficult, and whether those communications caused other damages to the plaintiffs.. Moreover, as to the claim of slander of title, while there is no evidence, that defendant, directly or by conspiring with others, slandered the title to the plaintiffs' entities, the fourth claim alleges disparagement of plaintiffs' title to property owned by them, and there is a triable issue on that point. Plaintiffs are directed to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the foregoing ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN D. O'GUINN VS. UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Declarating Pltf The Prevailing Party
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 29, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Declarating Pltf The Prevailing Party IS Denied. "Where an action has been voluntarily dismissed or dismissed pursuant to a settlement of the case, there shall be no prevailing party for purposes of this section." (Civil Code 1717(b)(2)) The foregoing is illustrative of California public policy against an award of fees where the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed the action, at least where, as here, no dispositive motion was pending. The statutes cited by defendant in its motion are not advanced by an award of fees to a defendant where the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed the action, especially when the plaintiff is pro per. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Award Of Supplemental Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 29, 2012, Line 12, PETITIONERS COALITION FOR ADEQUATE REVIEW, NINTY-NINE PERCENT AND ROB ANDERSON'S MOTION For Award Of Supplemental Attorney'S Fees is Granted. Petitioners are awarded all fees sought for all of Mr. Pearl's time at the requested rate without a multiplier and all hours spent by Ms. Miles for the first and second motions and this motion at $200 per hour. All other fees and costs requests are denied. Petitioners are not the prevailing parties on the third motion. It was not reasonable for petitioners to incur fees for the writ filed by Mr. Hayes and, even if it was, it is not reasonable to assess those fees against the City. $200 per hour is a reasonable rate for someone with Ms. Miles' experience, the large amount of time she spent on these motions and the fact that she performed functions that in another law office would likely have been performed by a secretary, clerk and/or paralegal. Given the nature of the motions and the large amount of time spent by Ms. Miles, a multiplier is inappropriate. Petitioners have not shown that, on this motion as opposed to a motion dealing with a costs memorandum, they are entitled to any of the costs they seek. The parties are directed to meet and confer to arrive at a dollar amount based on the foregoing rulings. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: SHERAE DRAKE et al VS. JOHN STEWART COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Motion To Dismiss Class Allegations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, February 29, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT JOHN STEWART COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION MOTION Motion To Dismiss Class Allegations IS Denied without prejudice to being re-filed on or after July 2, 2012 if Plaintiffs have not, by that date, filed a motion for class certification. Balancing the interests of the parties, while the court exercises its discretion not to hear the motion at this time, the timing of the determination of the propriety of class certification is not solely left to plaintiffs' counsel. Given that the case has been pending over a year, the next four months should afford both sides ample opportunity to do what is needed to have the propriety of class certification determined on a full evidentiary record. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: R. R. VS. EL TRABAJO INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set No. One And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday February 29, 2012 Line 2 - PLAINTIFF R. R.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set No. One And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Richard Grotch, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted; no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to RGrotch@CHDLAWYERS.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: R. R. VS. EL TRABAJO INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories To Defendant Edward Alkhasov, Set No. One And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday February 29, 2012 Line 2 - PLAINTIFF R. R.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories To Defendant Edward Alkhasov, Set No. One And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Richard Grotch, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted; no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to RGrotch@CHDLAWYERS.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KRISTINE ANDRES VS. RITA KILLILEA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Compelling Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctiosn
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday February 29, 2012 Line 3 - DEFENDANT RITA KILLILEA, MOIRA MCALISTER's Motion Compelling Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctios. Pro Tem Judge Richard Grotch, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' unopposed motion to compel is granted. Plaintiff shall serve a verified response to defendants' request for production, set one, without objections, and shall produce responsive documents to defendant within 15 days of notice of this ruling. Defendants' request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $200, which is to be paid by plaintiff to defendant within 15 days of notice of this ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to RGrotch@CHDLAWYERS.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)
